title: Ono marama – Te Marautanga o Aotearoa - Ministry of Education
content:
## Ono marama – Te Marautanga o Aotearoa
Starting kura is a big milestone for mokopuna. Here is some helpful information to support their first 6 months at kura.
- Guide to Te Marautanga o Aotearoa – Tau 0 to 8
- Ono marama – Te Marautanga o Aotearoa Te Reo Rangatira in the first 6 months Practising sounds and letters in the first 6 months Reading books at home in the first 6 months
- Te Reo Rangatira in the first 6 months
- Practising sounds and letters in the first 6 months
- Reading books at home in the first 6 months

- Tau 2 – Te Marautanga o Aotearoa

We’re inviting whānau to share their feedback on the Parent Portal to help us improve the information and support we provide. Take a few minutes to complete our short survey.
Parent Portal survey – Survey Monkey
- Te Reo Rangatira in the first 6 months Tēnā koutou. Nei rā ngā hau o mihi e pupuhi ana! Hei tautāwhi tēnei rauemi i a koutou ki te hāpai i te ako a ā tātou mokopuna i Te Reo Rangatira i te mutunga o ngā marama e ono ki te kura. Find out more

### Te Reo Rangatira in the first 6 months
Tēnā koutou. Nei rā ngā hau o mihi e pupuhi ana! Hei tautāwhi tēnei rauemi i a koutou ki te hāpai i te ako a ā tātou mokopuna i Te Reo Rangatira i te mutunga o ngā marama e ono ki te kura.
- Practising sounds and letters in the first 6 months Use these slides to help your mokopuna recognise letters and sounds by looking at pictures and words. Find out more

### Practising sounds and letters in the first 6 months
Use these slides to help your mokopuna recognise letters and sounds by looking at pictures and words.
- Reading books at home in the first 6 months Find out about supporting your child’s reading in the first 6 months of kura. Find out more
